item.title: A Tragic Case That Changed The Conversation
item.description: In 2004, Dena Schlosser became the focus of a widely publicized Texas investigation after the death of her infant daughter. The case centered on severe mental illness, competency, and criminal responsibility. She was later found not guilty by reason of insanity and committed to a state mental health facility.

media:content.media:title: A Tragic Case That Changed The Conversation
media:content.media:description: In 2004, Dena Schlosser became the focus of a widely publicized Texas investigation after the death of her infant daughter. The case centered on severe mental illness, competency, and criminal responsibility. She was later found not guilty by reason of insanity and committed to a state mental health facility.

item.title: The Shocking Twist In The Gumi Baby Mystery
item.description: The Gumi Baby case stunned South Korea in 2021 after DNA testing revealed an unexpected family connection that dramatically changed the direction of the investigation. As authorities worked to determine the child's identity and what had happened, the case raised complex questions about family relationships, evidence, and accountability that continue to draw public attention.

media:content.media:title: The Shocking Twist In The Gumi Baby Mystery
media:content.media:description: The Gumi Baby case stunned South Korea in 2021 after DNA testing revealed an unexpected family connection that dramatically changed the direction of the investigation. As authorities worked to determine the child's identity and what had happened, the case raised complex questions about family relationships, evidence, and accountability that continue to draw public attention.

item.title: How A Routine Morning Ended In Tragedy
item.description: In 1994, Seoul's Seongsu Bridge suddenly collapsed during the morning rush hour, claiming 32 lives and injuring many others. Investigators determined that poor construction practices and inadequate maintenance played key roles in the disaster. The collapse prompted sweeping changes to infrastructure inspections and public safety standards across South Korea.

media:content.media:title: How A Routine Morning Ended In Tragedy
media:content.media:description: In 1994, Seoul's Seongsu Bridge suddenly collapsed during the morning rush hour, claiming 32 lives and injuring many others. Investigators determined that poor construction practices and inadequate maintenance played key roles in the disaster. The collapse prompted sweeping changes to infrastructure inspections and public safety standards across South Korea.

item.title: Lindsay Buziak Walked Into A House And Never Came Out
item.description: In 2008, Canadian real estate agent Lindsay Buziak was lured to a home showing in Saanich, British Columbia, by two people posing as prospective buyers. The carefully planned case remains unsolved despite years of investigation, new forensic reviews, and continued appeals for information from authorities.

media:content.media:title: Lindsay Buziak Walked Into A House And Never Came Out
media:content.media:description: In 2008, Canadian real estate agent Lindsay Buziak was lured to a home showing in Saanich, British Columbia, by two people posing as prospective buyers. The carefully planned case remains unsolved despite years of investigation, new forensic reviews, and continued appeals for information from authorities.

item.title: How The Illegal Organ Trade Became A Billion Dollar Industry
item.description: The illegal organ trade is estimated to generate up to $1.7 billion annually, exploiting vulnerable people through trafficking, fraud, and unregulated medical procedures. This video examines how these networks operate, the challenges investigators face, and the international efforts to combat one of the world's most profitable illicit industries.

media:content.media:title: How The Illegal Organ Trade Became A Billion Dollar Industry
media:content.media:description: The illegal organ trade is estimated to generate up to $1.7 billion annually, exploiting vulnerable people through trafficking, fraud, and unregulated medical procedures. This video examines how these networks operate, the challenges investigators face, and the international efforts to combat one of the world's most profitable illicit industries.

item.title: The Nurse At The Center Of One Of Britain’s Biggest Criminal Cases
item.description: Lucy Letby, a neonatal nurse in England, was convicted in 2023 after a lengthy investigation into a series of infant deaths and unexplained medical collapses at the hospital where she worked. During the trial, prosecutors argued that she sought attention from a senior doctor, though Letby denied the allegations. Her convictions remain the subject of ongoing legal proceedings and public debate.

media:content.media:title: The Nurse At The Center Of One Of Britain’s Biggest Criminal Cases
media:content.media:description: Lucy Letby, a neonatal nurse in England, was convicted in 2023 after a lengthy investigation into a series of infant deaths and unexplained medical collapses at the hospital where she worked. During the trial, prosecutors argued that she sought attention from a senior doctor, though Letby denied the allegations. Her convictions remain the subject of ongoing legal proceedings and public debate.
